verdicts.note: The record says exists='yes', but the evidence shows only an announcement of a 'client preview starting July 2026' (releases page: 'DealCloud now provides a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server... available as part of the DealCloud with Celeste license'). This is DealCloud's own MCP server inside their product, not a dedicated third-party MCP server repository that developers can use to build integrations. An MCP server *inside* the vendor's product is not the same as an *existing* standalone MCP server for developers to use.

The URL https://api.docs.dealcloud.com/releases points to DealCloud's own release notes, not a standalone MCP server repository. This is not an MCP server developers can use; it's just an announcement page.

The one_liner states 'official MCP server in client preview as of mid-2026' which is misleading. The evidence shows the MCP server is *part of* DealCloud (requiring a 'DealCloud with Celeste' license) and in preview starting July 2026, not a standalone open MCP server for developers. The releases page says 'DealCloud now provides a Model Context Protocol (MCP) server which gives AI agents read access to your DealCloud data' - this is an internal feature, not an external developer tool.

Evidence clearly supports OAuth2 Client Credentials flow (POST to /api/rest/v1/oauth/token with client_id, apiKey/client_secret, scope(s); returns bearer token valid 900s) and the use of apiKey as client_secret. Both auth_methods entries are accurate.

Evidence confirms admin-approval model: apikeys doc shows 'Navigate to Admin > User Management... enable API' and 'click User icon... Profile... Enable' API key. No public self-serve signup mentioned; requires existing DealCloud subscription and admin permissions.
Accurately describes the admin-issued client_id/apiKey model and enterprise requirement per the apikeys and token documentation.
Evidence clearly describes REST APIs across multiple functional areas (token, user management, schema, data, backups, publications, relationship intelligence).
Evidence supports 'broad': multiple functional areas (User Management, Schema, Data, Backups, Publications, Relationship Intelligence), Swagger/OpenAPI spec, scopes for different endpoints.
Matches the evidence from /docs page listing the functional areas and Swagger availability.
buildable-with-caveats is appropriate: REST API is fully functional but requires enterprise subscription and admin-issued credentials.

Accurately describes the enterprise subscription requirement and admin-issued credentials. The mention of MCP server preview is reasonable context given the one_liner mentioned it, though the MCP server is not directly relevant to REST API buildability.
All four evidence URLs are present in the provided evidence and the 'supports' descriptions accurately reflect the content.
0.7 is reasonable given the clear API documentation but enterprise-only access model.

First two flags are supported by evidence. The third flag mentions 'Third-party MCP integrations exist via Intapp Celeste (limited release) and Louisa AI' which is not directly evidenced, but the releases page does mention 'DealCloud with Celeste' license, so partially supported.

revised.name: DealCloud
revised.one_liner: DealCloud is an enterprise deal/relationship CRM offering a broad REST API (data, schema, user management, publications, backups) secured via OAuth2 client credentials for developers with an existing subscription and admin-issued credentials.

auth_methods.notes: Client Credentials flow; POST client_id, apiKey/client_secret, and scope(s) to /api/rest/v1/oauth/token; returns bearer token valid 900 seconds, used in Authorization header.
apiKey used as the client_secret in the OAuth2 client credentials request; also referenced as standalone API Keys doc.

revised.self_serve_notes: No free tier or public self-serve signup; DealCloud is an enterprise CRM requiring an existing subscription/enterprise agreement. Developers get client_id/apiKey via their DealCloud site admin, not an open developer portal.

api_surface.notes: REST APIs organized into functional areas: User Management, Schema, Data, Backups, Publications, Relationship Intelligence, plus Swagger/OpenAPI spec exposed per-site.

revised.main_blocker: Requires an existing enterprise DealCloud subscription and admin-issued client_id/apiKey; no open self-serve developer signup.

evidence.supports: OAuth2 client credentials flow, scopes, 900s token expiry, Authorization header usage
Overview of REST API functional areas: auth, user management, schema, data, backups, publications, relationship intelligence; Swagger/Postman/VSCode tooling
Announcement of DealCloud MCP Server client preview starting July 2026

flags: No public free-tier/self-serve signup found — enterprise agreement required
DealCloud provides an internal MCP server (part of 'DealCloud with Celeste' license) in client preview as of July 2026, but this is not a standalone open MCP server for external developers
